MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-16-25 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MAY-16-2025 HEARING RE: PETITIONER ANDREW LINTON'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ON PETITIONER ANDREW LINTON'S SUBPOENA IS DENIED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1212. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)
REFUND/REIMBURSEMENT OF $435.00 FOR TRANSACTION (), PAYABLE TO LAW OFFICES OF MARK J. ROMEO, WARRANT #20009282, DATED JUN-13-2025

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Off Calendar

PETITIONER ANDREW LINTON'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)

Off Calendar May-16-2025 Off Calendar

To Compel Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER ANDREW LINTON's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to compel production of documents on petitioner Andrew Linton's subpoena is denied.

Linton has filed a petition for purposes of conducting discovery in aid of enforcing a federal judgment. He has not served a summons or the petition on any party. He now moves to enforce his deposition subpoena for the production of documents against the Law Offices of Mark Romeo. Romeo opposes the motion.

The Sister State Money Judgments Act does not apply to federal judgments. (See Code Civ. Proc. 1710.10, subd. (c), & comment; see also Rutter Group Cal. Prac. Guide Enf. J. & Debt Ch. 6J-2 ["The SSMJA does not apply to federal court judgments. . . . (Instead, nearly all federal court judgments are enforceable by registration in a California federal district . . . .)"].) Linton does not make clear the source of law that he contends authorizes him to enforce his federal judgment in this case. Romeo's opposition cites cases for the proposition that federal courts have the jurisdiction to enforce their own judgments (which this court does not doubt) but then says conclusorily that federal courts' ancillary jurisdiction to enforce their own judgments is exclusive. That is a different proposition for which Romeo does not present direct authority. Romeo also contends that bankruptcy courts have exclusive jurisdiction over bankruptcy matters. This is also a proposition this court readily accepts, but Romeo does not explain the relationship between this judgment and the bankruptcy proceedings from which the judgment arises. The court looks forward to more extensive future briefing should this issue recur.

Regardless, the court denies the motion. The subpoena seeks records of a consumer and was not served in the manner required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1985.3. Linton acknowledges as much and purports to have cured the issue on reply. But a party must present its supporting evidence in its moving papers, not on reply. Moreover, a defect of service means that objections to the deposition subpoena are not waived, yet Linton's motion is premised on the waiver of objections. Romeo contends that the subpoena has other procedural defects. The court does not reach all of them because it concludes that the failure of consumer notice alone requires denying the motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
